name: Mare de Déu de la Roca Loop
name_original: El Areny - Vuelta circular por la Mare de déu de la Roca
description: Hike the moderate 6.0-mile Mare de Déu de la Roca Loop for stunning Costa Dorada views and the iconic Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca.
region_intro: Hiking around Mont-Roig Del Camp offers diverse landscapes, from striking red rocky mountains to a varied coastline. The region is situated at the foot of the Llaberia and Prades mountain ranges, providing elevated views of the Camp de Tarragona. Its unique geological features include reddish sandstone formations and intriguing caves. This combination of mountains and sea creates a wide array of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Mont-Roig Del Camp The most popular hiking route is Mare de Déu de la Roca Loop, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the iconic Mare de Déu de la Roca hermitage, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Les Escaletes – Midday Rock loop from Vilanova d'Escornalbou, a moderate 3.3 miles (5.3 km) path. This trail features varied terrain with views towards the Midday Rock and surrounding mountains. Local hikers also love the View of Mare de Déu de la Roca – Midday Rock loop from Mont-roig del Camp, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through the distinctive red rock landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Mont-Roig Del Camp is defined by dramatic red rock mountains, coastal paths, and routes through the Llaberia and Prades mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Mont-Roig Del Camp are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 260 reviews. More than 1800 hikers have used komoot to explore Mont-Roig Del Camp's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Mont-Roig Del Camp?
A: Mont-Roig Del Camp offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 90 trails available. These routes cater to different preferences, from easy strolls to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What makes hiking in Mont-Roig Del Camp unique?
A: The region is distinctive for its dramatic red rocky mountains, which inspired artist Joan Miró, and its diverse landscapes that blend mountainous terrain with a beautiful coastline. You'll find a captivating mix of natural features, from intriguing caves and sandstone formations to panoramic sea views.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in Mont-Roig Del Camp?
A: Yes, Mont-Roig Del Camp has nearly 50 easy hiking trails, many of which are suitable for families. These routes often feature gentle gradients and offer opportunities to explore the diverse landscapes without significant challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40348221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenal Beach – Platja de l'Arenal loop from l'Hospitalet de l'Infant&lt;/a&gt; is an easy coastal walk.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Mont-Roig Del Camp?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Mont-Roig Del Camp are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular loop trails include the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951864723/mare-de-deu-de-la-roca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mare de Déu de la Roca Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1675605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Escaletes – Midday Rock loop from Vilanova d'Escornalbou&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/705436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Mare de Déu de la Roca – Midday Rock loop from Mont-roig del Camp&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes?
A: Mont-Roig Del Camp is known for its striking natural features. You'll encounter the region's namesake 'red mountain' with its reddish sandstone rocks, particularly around the Mare de Déu de la Roca hermitage. The area also boasts intriguing natural caves and unique rock formations, especially in the Muntanya de l'Areny. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5318117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foradada Cave – Midday Rock loop from Roca del Pagès&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to explore some of these formations.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to visit while hiking?
A: Absolutely. A prominent landmark is the 13th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3064827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca&lt;/a&gt;, perched on a red rock mountain, offering impressive panoramic views. The area also has a strong connection to artist Joan Miró, who found inspiration in its landscapes. You can also explore nearby castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mont-Roig Del Camp?
A: The hiking trails in Mont-Roig Del Camp are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 260 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the stunning views from elevated points like the Mare de Déu de la Roca, and the variety of routes available for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any coastal walks or trails with sea views?
A: Yes, Mont-Roig Del Camp boasts 12 kilometers of coastline with long, sandy beaches and quieter coves. You can find trails that offer beautiful sea views and coastal breezes, providing a different experience from the inland mountain routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40348221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenal Beach – Platja de l'Arenal loop from l'Hospitalet de l'Infant&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a coastal trail.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mont-Roig Del Camp?
A: The best time for hiking in Mont-Roig Del Camp is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is pleasantly mild, making it ideal for exploring both the mountainous terrain and coastal paths. Summers can be hot, especially for longer or more exposed routes.

Q: Are there trails that lead to interesting viewpoints?
A: Many trails in Mont-Roig Del Camp offer spectacular viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3064827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing extensive panoramic views of the Costa Dorada. Other notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5230652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Platja del Torn&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3063831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de la Rojala&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2375269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-Redon Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Mont-Roig Del Camp?
A: Mont-Roig Del Camp offers trails for all abilities. You'll find a good mix of options, with approximately 49 easy routes, 40 moderate routes, and 3 more challenging routes. This ensures that both casual walkers and experienced hikers can find suitable paths.
tips:
The hermitage of the Mare de Déu de la Roca offers a lodging service where the hermits who take care of it, have a restaurant service and room rental.

Around the natural area of the hermitage, there are several fountains and signposted spaces where you can eat and rest outdoors. It can be easily accessed by vehicle from the T-322 or on foot along various paths such as the GR-192, a section of which is also called Camino Viejo de Ia Ermita. Taking it, Mont-roig is reached in about 25 minutes on foot.

Masses: First Saturday of the month at 10:30 a.m.
Roquissar in the ridges of Areny on the mountain of Mare de deu de la Roca
A steep climb, for which you are rewarded with a magnificent view.
Barranco without more, passes through a centennial bridge.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Mare de Déu de la Roca Loop?
faqs.answer: The Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca is a common starting point and is accessible by vehicle. You can also find access points via paths like the GR-192, also known as Camino Viejo de Ia Ermita.

faqs.question: Is the Mare de Déu de la Roca Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally open to dogs, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the moderate difficulty and potential for steep sections. Be mindful of other hikers and any local regulations around the hermitage area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail offers rewarding scenery year-round. However, spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during warmer months. Winter hiking is possible, but check weather conditions for potential cold or wet spells.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Mare de Déu de la Roca Loop?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Mare de Déu de la Roca Loop. It is located within the Espai d'Interès Natural de la Mare de Déu de la Roca, which is freely accessible.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Mare de Déu de la Roca Loop is generally considered a moderate hike. While many paths are accessible, some sections, particularly those leading to the hermitage, can be steep. Good fitness and sure-footedness are recommended, but shorter options are available for less experienced hikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter varied terrain, from accessible paths to steeper sections that may require good footing. Some longer variations of the trail can even include sections equipped with ropes and cables, adding to the challenge.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca, perched on a rock promontory. You'll also pass the Natural Rock Arch of Mare de Déu de la Roca, Les Escaletes, and the Barranc de Rifà with its Rifà Bridge. The views of the Costa Dorada become increasingly magnificent as you climb.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Les Arrabassades, Les Pedreres, Camí del Vent, Mare de Deu de la Roca, Ermita del Peiró, Sender dels Carlins, TransCatalunya. De la Mediterrània, Els Estels, Les Planes, Camí vell de l'Ermita de la Roca, Carrerada de Coll Roig, GR-192, Cresta de l'Areny, Camí de la Font d'en Soler, Camí dels Masos d'en Briva, Camí de l'Aufinac GR-192, and Camí de Coll Roig trails.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca offers lodging, a restaurant, and room rentals. There are also various fountains and picnic areas nearby for your convenience.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, sun protection, and a map or GPS device. If you plan to explore longer, more challenging variations, consider trekking poles and be aware of sections that might have ropes or cables.

faqs.question: Are there any insider tips for making the most of the hike?
faqs.answer: To fully appreciate the views, consider starting your hike early in the morning, especially during warmer months, to avoid the midday heat and enjoy clearer vistas. For those interested in a more extensive exploration, look for variations that include points of interest like the Cova Foradada or the 'Escaleras del Diablo' (Devil's Stairs).

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca
